name: Castillo de Montesquiu and Besora Castle
name_original: Castillo de Montesquiu y castillo de Besora
description:
The historical and heritage importance of the Montesquiu and Besora castles is undeniable. And with this itinerary, you can explore both, as well as the surrounding landscapes, which are spectacular at any time of year.

The route begins and ends at the parking lot of the 9th-century Montesquiu Castle. It's open to the public and can be visited, but you must book in advance. Once underway, the path offers incredible panoramic views before reaching the most spectacular section of the trail: the Mongia Pass. This spectacular pass runs through marl, a reddish carbonate sedimentary rock containing many marine fossils.

A little further on, you'll spot the other star of this adventure: Besora Castle. Visit its ruins and those of the Romanesque church of Santa Maria, and enjoy the privileged views from the top of the hill where it stands. Very close by is Santa María de Besora, where you'll find the necessary services to recharge your batteries before returning to the point where you started the Route.
region_intro: Hiking around Montesquiu offers diverse natural landscapes, characterized by east-west mountain ranges like the Bufadors Range and the Serrat de la Rovira. The terrain features dense forests of oaks and Scots pines, riparian vegetation along waterways, and cultivated fields. Elevations in the area range between 560 and 885 meters above sea level, with the Ter River bisecting the park. Best hiking trails around Montesquiu The most popular hiking route is Castillo de Montesquiu and Besora Castle, a 7.1 miles (11.5 km) trail that takes 3 hours 33 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers panoramic views and historical sites. Another top favourite among local hikers is Serra dels Bufadors Trail, a moderate 7.8 miles (12.5 km) path. This trail winds through diverse forests and unique geological formations. Local hikers also love the Sant Moí Hermitage loop from Montesquiu, a 6.4 miles (10.3 km) trail leading through varied woodlands and past the historic hermitage, often completed in about 2 hours 59 minutes. Hiking around Montesquiu is defined by dense forests, river valleys, and varied mountain ranges, offering a network of routes suitable for different ability levels. The routes in Montesquiu are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from more than 200 reviews. More than 2000 hikers have used komoot to explore Montesquiu's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Montesquiu?
A: Montesquiu offers a diverse network of over 10 hiking trails. These routes cater to various fitness levels, with 3 easy trails and 9 moderate options, ensuring there's something for everyone to explore the region's natural beauty.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about Montesquiu?
A: The komoot community highly rates hiking in Montesquiu, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 240 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the diverse landscapes, which include dense forests, river valleys, and varied mountain ranges, as well as the historical sites like Montesquiu Castle and the Sant Moí Hermitage.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families in Montesquiu?
A: Yes, Montesquiu features 3 easy hiking trails perfect for beginners or families. These paths allow you to enjoy the region's natural beauty without requiring advanced fitness or experience. The area's varied terrain ensures accessible options alongside more challenging routes.

Q: Can I find hikes with scenic viewpoints in Montesquiu?
A: Absolutely. Montesquiu is known for its scenic vistas. The region research highlights viewpoints such as the Montesquiu Viewpoint, offering views of the village and the Ter River, and La Rovira Viewpoint, which provides expansive views of forests with red pine, oak, and beech trees. Many trails, including the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1734211968/castillo-de-montesquiu-and-besora-castle" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Montesquiu and Besora Castle&lt;/a&gt; route, offer panoramic views.

Q: Are there any waterfalls to see on hikes in Montesquiu?
A: Yes, the Montesquiu area features beautiful waterfalls that can be discovered during your hikes. Notable examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3035235" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vidrà Mill Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5414946" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mir Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, both of which are listed as natural monuments in the region.

Q: What historical or cultural attractions can I explore while hiking in Montesquiu?
A: Montesquiu is rich in historical and cultural sites. You can explore the Montesquiu Castle, a large fortified house dating back to the 10th century, which gives the natural park its name. Other points of interest include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14952502" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sant Moí Hermitage&lt;/a&gt;, old farmhouses, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2261304" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bellmunt Sanctuary&lt;/a&gt;. These attractions are often integrated into popular hiking loops, offering a blend of nature and history.

Q: Are there any natural monuments or unique geological features to discover?
A: Yes, Montesquiu boasts unique natural monuments and geological formations. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e985139242/serra-dels-bufadors-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Serra dels Bufadors Trail&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, winds through diverse forests and unique geological features, including the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/741468" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Els Bufadors de Beví&lt;/a&gt;. You can also find the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4326401" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Lady's Oak&lt;/a&gt;, a significant natural landmark.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Montesquiu?
A: Yes, Montesquiu offers several circular hiking routes. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14952502" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sant Moí Hermitage loop from Montesquiu&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through varied woodlands and past the historic hermitage. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31902486" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sant Moí Hermitage – Els Bufadors de Beví loop from La Farga de Bebié&lt;/a&gt;, providing a comprehensive exploration of the area's natural and historical highlights.

Q: What is the best time of year to hike in Montesquiu?
A: Montesquiu offers pleasant hiking conditions through much of the year. The region's diverse forests and varied terrain make it enjoyable in spring and autumn for comfortable temperatures and vibrant foliage. Even in winter, the trails can be accessible, though conditions may vary, especially at higher elevations.

Q: Is Montesquiu suitable for dog-friendly hikes?
A: While specific restrictions for dogs are not detailed, Montesquiu's natural park setting and numerous trails generally welcome dogs, especially on leash. It's always advisable to check local signage or park regulations upon arrival to ensure a pleasant experience for both you and your canine companion.

Q: How can I get to the Montesquiu hiking trails using public transport?
A: Information regarding specific public transport links directly to the trailheads in Montesquiu is not readily available. However, Montesquiu is a village, and local or regional bus services might connect it to larger towns. For detailed public transport options, it is recommended to consult local transport authorities or plan your journey to the village itself.

Q: Where can I park when hiking in Montesquiu?
A: While specific parking areas for individual trailheads are not detailed, Montesquiu, as a natural park and village, typically offers designated parking facilities for visitors. It's advisable to look for parking near the Montesquiu Castle or the main access points to the natural park, which often serve as starting points for many trails.
tips: The Montesquiu castle, in the Osona region, is a beautiful example of a recently restored medieval fortification. Built, surely, during the 13th century, it formed a defensive triangle together with the castles of Besora and Saderra.

The ruins of the Besora castle and the Romanesque church of Santa María are located on a hill at more than 1000 meters above sea level. It is worth calmly exploring each of its corners and enjoying the beautiful views.
Santa María de Besora is a beautiful town in the Osona area. It is surrounded by mountains and has an 11th century church. There are some good hikes that start from here.
